target.title: fix(doctor): suppress false-positive group allowlist warning for parent-only channel records (#92684)
target.body:
## Summary

Fix false-positive doctor warning: when a channel has per-account configs with
populated `groupAllowFrom` lists, the empty top-level `groupAllowFrom` no longer
triggers a misleading "all group messages will be silently dropped" warning.

## Root Cause

`scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` called `checkAccount` on the top-level
channel record as if it were a standalone account — even when active per-account
sub-records existed. When the top-level `groupAllowFrom` was empty (serving only
as a fallback), the doctor treated it as an unconfigured account and emitted the
group-drop warning. In reality, every account supplied its own populated
`groupAllowFrom`, so the top-level empty list was never read by the runtime.

Two code defects were present in the initial fix:

1. **TS2345 type error**: `asObjectRecord()` returns `Record<string, unknown> | null`,
   so `accounts && Object.values(...).some(...)` yields `boolean | null` rather than
   `boolean`. Fixed with `Boolean()` coercion.
2. **Plugin extra-warning path not gated**: `suppressGroupWarning` only wrapped the
   shared default warning hook via `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning`,
   but Telegram channels skip the default hook and emit group-allowlist warnings
   through `extraWarningsForAccount` instead. The parent-scope extra-warning call
   was not gated, leaving the Telegram false-positive path reachable. Fixed by
   gating `extraWarningsForAccount` under `!suppressGroupWarning`.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior
Doctor no longer emits a false-positive group-allowlist warning for an empty
top-level `groupAllowFrom` when every per-account record carries its own
populated allowlist. The plugin extra-warning path (Telegram) is also gated.
Non-regression: DM allowlist warnings still fire on parent records; group
warnings still fire when no per-account configs exist.

### environment
- OS: Windows 10 Enterprise LTSC 2019
- Runtime: Node.js 22.x, tsx (direct TypeScript execution, no vitest, no mocks)
- Setup: openclaw workspace at fix commit (52ae4a23), production source modules

### steps
1. Configure a channel with `groupPolicy: "allowlist"`, empty top-level
   `groupAllowFrom`, and accounts each with populated `groupAllowFrom`.
2. Import `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` directly from production source via tsx.
3. Call the scanner with the config; observe 0 warnings.
4. Verify non-regression: parent-only channel still warns; DM allowlist warning
   still fires on parent records.

### observedResult

**Direct scanner invocation via tsx (production source, no vitest):**
```
$ ./node_modules/.bin/tsx scripts/repro-92684-proof.mjs

[1/4] importing scanner...
[2/4] scanner imported
[3/4] Scenario 1: parent + populated per-account allowlists
  done in 4799ms, 0 warning(s) (expected: 0)
[4/4] Scenario 2: parent-only, no accounts
  1 warning(s) (expected: 1)
    - channels.telegram.groupPolicy is "allowlist" but groupAllowFrom
      (and allowFrom) is empty — all group messages will be silently
      dropped. Add sender IDs to channels.telegram.groupAllowFrom or
      channels.telegram.allowFrom, or set groupPolicy to "open".
       Scenario 3: DM allowlist still fires on parent
  1 warning(s) (expected: 1, DM only)
    - channels.telegram.dmPolicy is "allowlist" but allowFrom is
      empty — all DMs will be blocked. Add sender IDs to
      channels.telegram.allowFrom, or run "openclaw doctor --fix"
      to auto-migrate from pairing store when entries exist.

============================================================
  RESULTS
============================================================
  Scenario 1 (parent + accounts): PASS (got 0, expected 0)
  Scenario 2 (parent-only):       PASS (got 1, expected 1)
  Scenario 3 (DM preserved):      PASS (got 1, expected 1 DM)
  Overall: ALL PASS
============================================================
```

**Test suite (vitest):**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
```

All 16 existing doctor tests pass; no regressions.

**Production change (`src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.ts`):**
```diff
-    checkAccount(channelConfig, `channels.${channelName}`, channelName);
-
     const accounts = asObjectRecord(channelConfig.accounts);
+    const hasActiveAccounts = Boolean(
+      accounts &&
+        Object.values(accounts).some((a) => !isDisabledRecord(a)),
+    );
+
+    // When the channel has active per-account configs, the top-level record
+    // serves only as a parent fallback. Suppress the default
+    // empty-group-allowlist warning so an empty top-level groupAllowFrom
+    // does not produce a false positive when every account supplies its own
+    // populated allowlist (issue #92684).
+    checkAccount(
+      channelConfig,
+      `channels.${channelName}`,
+      channelName,
+      undefined,
+      hasActiveAccounts,
+    );
```

In `checkAccount`:
```diff
-    if (params.extraWarningsForAccount) {
+    if (!suppressGroupWarning && params.extraWarningsForAccount) {
```

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts` — 3 passed
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.test.ts` — 13 passed
- [x] `./node_modules/.bin/tsx scripts/repro-92684-proof.mjs` — 3/3 scenarios pass (direct scanner invocation)
- [x] Change is minimal (single file, 2 logical fixes)

---

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
target.author: sheyanmin
